164→     * to the EventBus from the ViewModel and we have to use `sticky` events instead.
165→     */
166→    @Subscribe(threadMode = ThreadMode.BACKGROUND, sticky = true)
167→    @Suppress("unused")
168→    fun onSiteCreationServiceStateUpdated(event: SiteCreationServiceState) {
169→        if (lastReceivedServiceState == event) return // filter out events which we've already received
170→        lastReceivedServiceState = event
171→        when (event.step) {
172→            IDLE, CREATE_SITE -> Unit
173→            SUCCESS -> {
174→                site = mapPayloadToSiteModel(event.payload)
175→
176→                val isFreePlan = siteCreationState.plan?.productSlug == "free_plan"
177→
178→                if (siteCreationState.plan == null || isFreePlan) {
179→                    _onFreeSiteCreated.postValue(site) // MainVM will navigate forward if the domain is free
180→                } else {
181→                    createCart()
182→                }
183→            }
184→
185→            FAILURE -> {
186→                serviceStateForRetry = event.payload as SiteCreationServiceState
187→                tracker.trackErrorShown(
188→                    ERROR_CONTEXT,
189→                    UNKNOWN,
190→                    "SiteCreation service failed"
191→                )
192→                updateUiStateAsync(GenericError)
193→            }
194→        }
195→    }
196→
197→    private fun mapPayloadToSiteModel(payload: Any?): SiteModel {
198→        require(payload is Pair<*, *>) { "Expected Pair in Payload, got: $payload" }
199→        val (blogId, blogUrl) = payload
200→        require(blogId is Long) { "Expected the 1st element in the Payload Pair to be a Long, got: $blogId" }
201→        require(blogUrl is String) { "Expected the 2nd element in the Payload Pair to be a Long, got: $blogUrl" }
202→        return SiteModel().apply { siteId = blogId; url = blogUrl }
203→    }
204→
205→    private fun createCart() = launch {
206→        AppLog.d(T.SITE_CREATION, "Creating cart: $domain")
207→
208→        val event = createCartUseCase.execute(
209→            site,
210→            domain.productId,
211→            domain.domainName,
212→            domain.supportsPrivacy,
213→            false,
214→            planProductId = siteCreationState.plan?.productId
215→        )
216→
217→        if (event.isError) {
218→            AppLog.e(T.SITE_CREATION, "Failed cart creation: ${event.error.message}")
219→            updateUiStateAsync(CartError)
220→        } else {
221→            AppLog.d(T.SITE_CREATION, "Successful cart creation: ${event.cartDetails}")
222→            _onCartCreated.postValue(CheckoutDetails(site, domain.domainName, showCloseButton = true))
223→        }
224→    }
225→
226→    private fun runLoadingAnimationUi() {
227→        loadingAnimationJob?.cancel()
228→        loadingAnimationJob = launch {
229→            loadingTexts.forEachIndexed { i, uiString ->
230→                updateUiState(
231→                    Loading(
232→                        animate = i != 0, // the first text should appear without an animation
233→                        loadingTextResId = uiString
234→                    )
235→                )
236→                delay(LOADING_STATE_TEXT_ANIMATION_DELAY)
237→            }
238→        }
239→    }
240→
241→    private fun updateUiState(uiState: SiteProgressUiState) {
242→        if (uiState !is Loading) loadingAnimationJob?.cancel()
243→        _uiState.value = uiState
244→    }
245→
246→    private fun updateUiStateAsync(uiState: SiteProgressUiState) {
247→        if (uiState !is Loading) loadingAnimationJob?.cancel()
248→        _uiState.postValue(uiState)
249→    }

252→        val progressLayoutVisibility: Boolean = false,
253→        val errorLayoutVisibility: Boolean = false
254→    ) {

16→import kotlin.coroutines.resume
17→import kotlin.coroutines.suspendCoroutine
18→
19→/**
20→ * Transforms OnNewSiteCreated EventBus event to a coroutine.
21→ */
22→class CreateSiteUseCase @Inject constructor(
23→    private val dispatcher: Dispatcher,
24→    @Suppress("unused") private val siteStore: SiteStore,
25→    private val urlUtilsWrapper: UrlUtilsWrapper
26→) {
27→    private var continuation: Continuation<OnNewSiteCreated>? = null
28→
29→    @Suppress("UseCheckOrError")
30→    suspend fun createSite(
31→        siteData: SiteCreationServiceData,
32→        languageWordPressId: String,
33→        timeZoneId: String,
34→        siteVisibility: SiteVisibility = PUBLIC,
35→        dryRun: Boolean = false
36→    ): OnNewSiteCreated {
37→        if (continuation != null) {
38→            throw IllegalStateException("Create site request has already been sent.")
39→        }
40→        /*
41→         * To create a site with WordPress.com sub-domain, we need to pass the domain name without the "wordpress.com"
42→         * whereas to create a site with other domains, we need to pass the full url. This issue is addressed
43→         * in this use case since it's closest to the network layer.
44→         *
45→         * Ideally API wouldn't work like this or if it does FluxC is the one that handles the issue. However, at the
46→         * time of this comment, changing FluxC's Payload might end up affecting the old site creation flow,
47→         * so the workaround is applied here instead.
48→         */
49→        val domain = when {
50→            siteData.domain.isNullOrEmpty() -> null
51→            isWordPressComSubDomain(siteData.domain) -> urlUtilsWrapper.extractSubDomain(siteData.domain)
52→            else -> siteData.domain
53→        }
54→        return suspendCoroutine { cont ->
55→            val newSitePayload = NewSitePayload(
56→                domain,
57→                siteData.title,
58→                languageWordPressId,
59→                timeZoneId,
60→                siteVisibility,
61→                siteData.segmentId,
62→                siteData.siteDesign,
63→                dryRun,
64→                findAvailableUrl = if (siteData.isFree) null else true
65→            )
66→            continuation = cont
67→            dispatcher.dispatch(SiteActionBuilder.newCreateNewSiteAction(newSitePayload))
68→        }
69→    }
70→
71→    @Suppress("unused")
72→    @Subscribe(threadMode = ThreadMode.BACKGROUND)
73→    fun onNewSiteCreated(event: OnNewSiteCreated) {
74→        continuation?.resume(event)
75→        continuation = null
76→    }
77→}
78→
79→fun isWordPressComSubDomain(url: String) = url.endsWith(".wordpress.com")
80→

content.thinking:
**Evaluating event handling**

I'm looking into the OnNewSiteCreated event and considering that a success event has both remoteId and a URL. The siteExists error in test sets seems related to the URL, but it’s unclear if that’s true for real-world scenarios. The earlier logs indicate a 400 error, which could cause failures. If the server does return a URL for a genuinely created site, we might need to adjust our conditions. It seems safer to check just the remoteId, which will allow for better handling of success states even if the URL is blank.**Simplifying success criteria**

I’ve realized that the additional URL check might not be necessary and could lead to false failures. So, I need to remove that condition. Instead, I should base our success solely on whether the remoteId is greater than 0; if it’s not, that results in a failure. This means I’ll need to revert the last patch and make those edits. Keeping things simple might be more effective for success tracking!

content.text:
Implemented a minimal fix to prevent the crash in `SiteCreationServiceManager.createSite()`:

- Removed the `throw IllegalStateException(...)` when the API returns `SITE_NAME_EXISTS` on the initial attempt, and instead transition the service to `FAILURE` (recoverable error state).
- Tightened the existing retry behavior: on retry, `SITE_NAME_EXISTS` is treated as `SUCCESS` **only if** the response includes a valid `newSiteRemoteId`; otherwise it’s treated as `FAILURE` to avoid falsely advancing the flow.

169→        if (lastReceivedServiceState == event) return // filter out events which we've already received
170→        lastReceivedServiceState = event
171→        when (event.step) {
172→            IDLE, CREATE_SITE -> Unit
173→            SUCCESS -> {
174→                site = mapPayloadToSiteModel(event.payload)
175→
176→                val isFreePlan = siteCreationState.plan?.productSlug == "free_plan"
177→
178→                if (siteCreationState.plan == null || isFreePlan) {
179→                    _onFreeSiteCreated.postValue(site) // MainVM will navigate forward if the domain is free
180→                } else {

183→            }
184→
185→            FAILURE -> {
186→                serviceStateForRetry = event.payload as SiteCreationServiceState
187→                tracker.trackErrorShown(

189→                    UNKNOWN,
190→                    "SiteCreation service failed"

19→/**
20→ * Transforms OnNewSiteCreated EventBus event to a coroutine.
21→ */
22→class CreateSiteUseCase @Inject constructor(
23→    private val dispatcher: Dispatcher,
24→    @Suppress("unused") private val siteStore: SiteStore,
25→    private val urlUtilsWrapper: UrlUtilsWrapper
26→) {
27→    private var continuation: Continuation<OnNewSiteCreated>? = null
28→
29→    @Suppress("UseCheckOrError")
30→    suspend fun createSite(
31→        siteData: SiteCreationServiceData,
32→        languageWordPressId: String,
33→        timeZoneId: String,
34→        siteVisibility: SiteVisibility = PUBLIC,
35→        dryRun: Boolean = false
36→    ): OnNewSiteCreated {
37→        if (continuation != null) {
38→            throw IllegalStateException("Create site request has already been sent.")
39→        }
40→        /*
41→         * To create a site with WordPress.com sub-domain, we need to pass the domain name without the "wordpress.com"
42→         * whereas to create a site with other domains, we need to pass the full url. This issue is addressed
43→         * in this use case since it's closest to the network layer.
44→         *
45→         * Ideally API wouldn't work like this or if it does FluxC is the one that handles the issue. However, at the
46→         * time of this comment, changing FluxC's Payload might end up affecting the old site creation flow,
47→         * so the workaround is applied here instead.
48→         */
49→        val domain = when {
50→            siteData.domain.isNullOrEmpty() -> null
51→            isWordPressComSubDomain(siteData.domain) -> urlUtilsWrapper.extractSubDomain(siteData.domain)
52→            else -> siteData.domain
53→        }
54→        return suspendCoroutine { cont ->
55→            val newSitePayload = NewSitePayload(
56→                domain,
57→                siteData.title,
58→                languageWordPressId,
59→                timeZoneId,
60→                siteVisibility,
61→                siteData.segmentId,
62→                siteData.siteDesign,
63→                dryRun,
64→                findAvailableUrl = if (siteData.isFree) null else true
65→            )
66→            continuation = cont
67→            dispatcher.dispatch(SiteActionBuilder.newCreateNewSiteAction(newSitePayload))
68→        }
69→    }
70→
71→    @Suppress("unused")
72→    @Subscribe(threadMode = ThreadMode.BACKGROUND)
73→    fun onNewSiteCreated(event: OnNewSiteCreated) {
74→        continuation?.resume(event)
75→        continuation = null
76→    }
77→}

79→fun isWordPressComSubDomain(url: String) = url.endsWith(".wordpress.com")
